functional_requirements.title: Stripe Payment Provider Integration
functional_requirements.description: Clinic admin configures Stripe API keys. Payment endpoint processes appointment payments securely via Stripe Elements (PCI compliant). Webhook receives payment events (success/failure).
acceptance_criteria: Stripe account created and API keys configured in environment
POST /api/payments/process endpoint created and tested
Card tokenization via Stripe.js (no card data stored locally)
Payment status saved to appointments table
Webhook /api/webhooks/stripe receives payment.success and payment.failed events
Error handling for declined cards, timeouts, idempotency duplicates
PCI DSS compliance audit passed
Test coverage >= 80% (happy path + error scenarios)

functional_requirements.title: Payment Method Storage & PCI Compliance
functional_requirements.description: Stripe Payment Method tokens stored securely in Supabase (no raw card data). Patients can save payment methods for future transactions. Audit logging captures all tokenization events.
acceptance_criteria: Stripe Payment Method tokens stored (payment_method_id only)
Saved payment methods listed in patient profile
DELETE endpoint for removing saved payment methods
Payment method selectable as default for future transactions
Audit log captures: user, operation, timestamp, success/failure
No card data ever touches application servers
RLS policies prevent cross-patient access to payment methods
External PCI audit (if required by clinic)

functional_requirements.title: Invoice Generation & Email Delivery
functional_requirements.description: On appointment completion, system automatically generates PDF invoice with clinic details, patient info, service description, amount, and date. Email sent to patient with invoice attached.
acceptance_criteria: Invoice PDF generated on appointment completion (trigger)
Invoice includes: clinic name/CNPJ, patient name/CPF, service details, amount, payment method, date
Email sent to patient automatically via configured email service
Invoice stored in audit log (path to PDF)
Invoice number unique and sequential (audit-friendly)
Email template customizable by clinic admin
Resend invoice endpoint available (manual retry)
Invoice preview available in patient dashboard

non_functional_requirements.title: PCI DSS Compliance (Level 1)
non_functional_requirements.description: Payment processing must be PCI Level 1 compliant. No raw card data stored or logged. Stripe handles tokenization.
non_functional_requirements.requirement: All payment operations must pass PCI DSS self-assessment (SAQ A or A-EP). Third-party audit may be required.

non_functional_requirements.title: Brazil Payment Methods
non_functional_requirements.description: Support Brazil-specific payment methods (PIX, boleto, credit card). Currency in BRL.
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Stripe must be configured for Brazil (currency BRL, payment methods: card, pix, boleto)

non_functional_requirements.title: Security & Webhook Reliability
non_functional_requirements.description: Webhook signature validation required. Retry logic for delivery failures. Idempotency to prevent duplicate charges.
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Webhook signature validated using Stripe-provided secret. Max 30s response time. Implement exponential backoff retry (1s, 5s, 30s).

non_functional_requirements.title: Performance
non_functional_requirements.description: Payment processing latency < 3 seconds. Invoice generation < 5 seconds.

non_functional_requirements.title: Audit Logging & LGPD Compliance
non_functional_requirements.description: All payment operations logged with full audit trail (user, operation, timestamp, result, amount, method). Compliant with Brazil LGPD (data retention, access control).
non_functional_requirements.requirement: Audit log immutable. Retain for 5+ years. Access restricted to clinic owner/accountant.

data_flows.name: Patient Payment Flow
steps: Patient enters card details (never touch backend)
Frontend tokenizes card → Stripe → returns payment_method_id
Frontend calls POST /api/payments/process { payment_method_id, amount, appointment_id }
Invoice PDF generated and emailed to patient

data_flows.name: Save Payment Method Flow
steps: Patient clicks 'Save this card for future use'
Backend stores payment_method_id in patients.saved_payment_methods table
Audit log records: 'payment_method_saved' with timestamp, user_id, payment_method_id (no card number)

data_flows.name: Invoice Generation & Email Flow
steps: Create invoice record in invoices table
Render HTML template with appointment + patient + clinic details
Convert HTML → PDF using html2pdf library
Upload PDF to cloud storage (or embed in email)
Send email to patient via Resend/SendGrid with PDF attachment

constraints: No raw card data can be stored or logged
Stripe Setup Intents must be used for tokenization
All webhook events must be idempotent (prevent duplicate processing)
Invoice generation must be triggered automatically (not manual)
Payment method deletion must cascade safely (no orphaned references)
Audit logs must be immutable and tamper-proof
Brazil tax compliance: invoices may need NF-e or RPS integration (deferred to Phase 3)
assumptions: Stripe account already exists (clinic provided credentials)
Supabase database schema already includes appointments, patients, audit_logs tables
Email service (Resend or SendGrid) already configured
Next.js 16+ with Edge Functions support available
LGPD compliance officer will review audit log schema
Clinic does NOT require NF-e (Brazilian tax document) in Phase 2 (deferred to Phase 3)
